name: El Salvador

visa_free.notes: Citizens of Guatemala, Honduras, and Nicaragua can enter El Salvador without a visa or passport using only their national ID card under the CA-4 Border Control Agreement (Convenio de Creación de la Visa Única Centroamericana, 2005). The 90-day period is shared across the entire CA-4 region (El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua) - a traveller who has spent time in any CA-4 country draws down from the same 90-day quota. El Salvador is the receiving country here; citizens of El Salvador itself are not counted. Costa Rica and Panama are NOT part of the CA-4 agreement.

El Salvador's immigration law (Ley Especial de Migración y Extranjería) establishes three entry categories: Category A (exento de visa – visa-exempt), Category B (visa consular o sin consulta), and Category C (visa consultada – requires prior DGME authorization). Category A visitors are admitted without a tourist entry card fee - the USD 12 Tarjeta de Ingreso Migratorio (TIM) was eliminated by the Legislative Assembly in April/May 2025. Standard stay is 180 days for Category A visitors (confirmed by UK GOV.UK and Canada travel.gc.ca official government sources, June 2026; Wikipedia Visa policy of El Salvador also states 180 days). Note: the CA-4 regional shared quota limits movement across the CA-4 bloc (El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua) to 90 days combined; the 180-day figure applies to El Salvador's own individual grant. The official country list is at https://www.migracion.gob.sv/servicios/listado-visa/ (downloadable Google Drive file, published 2020-11-10). Approximately 89 jurisdictions are Category A, including all 27 EU member states plus Albania, Andorra, Antigua and Barbuda, Argentina, Australia, Bahamas, Bahrain, Barbados, Belize, Bolivia, Brazil, Brunei, Canada, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, Fiji, Iceland, Israel, Japan, Kuwait, Liechtenstein, Madagascar, Malaysia, Marshall Islands, Mexico, Monaco, New Zealand, North Macedonia, Norway, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Qatar, Russia, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, San Marino, São Tomé and Príncipe, Saudi Arabia, Singapore, Solomon Islands, South Africa, South Korea, Switzerland, Trinidad and Tobago, Turkey, Tuvalu, Ukraine, UAE, United Kingdom, United States, Uruguay, Vanuatu, Vatican City. The authoritative source for the definitive list remains the official DGME downloadable document.

e_visa.nationality: Nationals requiring a visa who cannot obtain one through normal consular channels
e_visa.notes: El Salvador offers a Special Entry Permit (Permiso Especial de Ingreso, PEI) applied for online at https://elsalvadorpei.migracion.gob.sv/. Eligibility: foreign nationals who require a visa based on their nationality but cannot obtain one through a Salvadoran consulate due to lack of embassy presence, residency issues, force majeure, or unforeseen circumstances. Fee: USD 100. Process: create account, complete application, upload required documents, pay fee. Applicant receives approval, conditional approval, denial, or cancellation by email. Contact: +503 7070-0663 / atencion.usuarios@migracion.gob.sv. Service page last updated November 14, 2023.

visa_policy.notes: El Salvador uses three entry categories established under the Special Law on Migration and Foreigners (Ley Especial de Migración y Extranjería): Category A (exento de visa – visa-exempt), Category B (visa consular o sin consulta – consular visa obtainable without prior DGME approval), Category C (visa consultada – requires prior DGME authorization). The official downloadable list of countries is published at https://www.migracion.gob.sv/servicios/listado-visa/ (file hosted on Google Drive, published 2020-11-10). CA-4 agreement nationals (Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua) can enter with national ID only within a shared 90-day CA-4 regional quota. Category A visitors are admitted without paying a tourist entry card fee - the USD 12 Tarjeta de Ingreso Migratorio (TIM) fee was eliminated by the Legislative Assembly in April/May 2025. Standard stay for Category A is 180 days (confirmed by UK GOV.UK and Canada travel.gc.ca, June 2026). Note: The CA-4 shared 90-day quota applies to movement within the CA-4 bloc — El Salvador individually can grant up to 180 days; but time spent in other CA-4 countries counts toward the 90-day CA-4 shared total. The e-visa/PEI system is at https://elsalvadorpei.migracion.gob.sv/ for USD 100. El Salvador broke diplomatic relations with Taiwan in 2018 and now recognizes the People's Republic of China only.

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must possess a valid (unexpired) US visa. Nationals from the excluded list remain subject to Category C (consulted visa requiring prior DGME authorization) regardless of holding a US visa. The official RREE visa checker (rree.gob.sv/visas/) explicitly asks whether the traveller holds a valid US, Canada, or Schengen visa as a determining condition for entry category.

Holder must possess a valid (unexpired) Canadian visa. Nationals from the excluded list remain subject to Category C (consulted visa requiring prior DGME authorization) regardless of holding a Canadian visa. The official RREE visa checker (rree.gob.sv/visas/) explicitly asks whether the traveller holds a valid US, Canada, or Schengen visa as a determining condition for entry category.

Holder must possess a valid (unexpired) Schengen area visa. Nationals from the excluded list remain subject to Category C (consulted visa requiring prior DGME authorization) regardless of holding a Schengen visa. The official RREE visa checker (rree.gob.sv/visas/) explicitly asks whether the traveller holds a valid US, Canada, or Schengen visa as a determining condition for entry category.

The official RREE visa checker (rree.gob.sv/visas/) explicitly asks '¿Usted reside legalmente en otro país diferente al de su nacionalidad?' (Do you legally reside in another country different from your nationality?) as a separate determining condition for entry category. The specific issuing countries of qualifying residence permits and any excluded nationalities are determined dynamically by the official tool and are not enumerated in a static official document accessible at time of validation.

Holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports from the listed countries do not require a visa to enter El Salvador. Ordinary passport holders from these countries may face different requirements (Category B or C). The table on rree.gob.sv/visas-para-extranjeros/ only partially rendered during validation (rows A through Azerbaijan visible); this list was cross-referenced from the Wikipedia article on El Salvador visa policy which aggregates the RREE data.

Agreement between the Government of El Salvador and the Government of the Republic of India for the Suppression of the Visa Requirement for Bearers of Diplomatic and Official Passports. Signed 10 June 2008; entered into force 1 April 2009. Confirmed on the official India Ministry of External Affairs bilateral visa waiver agreements page.

The official RREE visa checker (rree.gob.sv/visas/) lists OEA (Organization of American States), Naciones Unidas (United Nations), and Comisión Europea (European Commission) as recognized passport-issuing entities alongside sovereign states, indicating holders of travel documents issued by these international organizations are subject to the visa-exemption framework. Specific conditions for each organization's travel documents were not enumerated on any static official page found.

cbi.program_name: Adopting El Salvador – Freedom Passport (Pasaporte de la Libertad)

options.notes: Total investment of USD 1,000,000 payable in Bitcoin (BTC) or Tether (USDt). Payment is split: a non-refundable application fee of USD 999 in BTC or USDt to commence evaluation, then USD 999,001 upon approval. Additional administrative fee of USD 999 per family member for family applications. Annual quota of 1,000 investors per year. Investment directed toward El Salvador's economic development, cultural enrichment, and social programs. Grants Salvadoran citizenship including right to vote. Reported processing time ~6 weeks. Spouse and dependent children under 18 may be included in one application.

cbi.processing_time: Approximately 6 weeks (as stated on official world.adoptingelsalvador.gob.sv)
cbi.notes: The program is branded 'Adopting El Salvador' and operated via the government domain world.adoptingelsalvador.gob.sv and adoptingelsalvador.gob.sv. The program is confirmed active as of mid-2026. Bitcoin lost mandatory legal-tender status in January 2025 but remains legal to use; the program now also accepts Tether (USDt) in addition to BTC. The Legislative Assembly separately approved reforms in May 2025 (57 votes) to Art. 152 of the Special Law on Migration and Foreigners, allowing foreigners who invest or donate in government-promoted programs to obtain definitive residence directly (skipping temporary residence), with a fee of USD 690. This reform covers residency rather than citizenship. Separately, 5,000 free passports for highly qualified scientists, engineers, doctors, artists, and philosophers were announced, but official details on that sub-program with specific eligibility criteria are not publicly documented.

programs.name: Definitive Residence for Foreign Investors (Art. 152 Reform)
programs.notes: Approved May 2025 by the Legislative Assembly with 57 votes (previously reported as 56 - corrected to 57 per official source). Foreigners who invest or donate in programs promoted by the Government can obtain definitive (permanent) residence directly without first holding temporary residence. Fee: USD 690. Requirements include clean criminal record, identity document, and proof of investment/donation in a government program. Minimum investment amount not specified in official sources. Family group of the main applicant may be included.

programs.name: Temporary Residence – Investor / Business Category
programs.notes: El Salvador's immigration authority offers temporary residence for investors and business persons as one of several qualifying categories. Specific minimum investment amounts were not published on the official page reviewed. Applicants submit application, required documents, and pay applicable fees. A temporary residence card is issued and is renewable.

programs.name: Special Entry Permit (Permiso Especial de Ingreso – PEI)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals who require a visa to enter El Salvador but cannot obtain one through normal consular channels (no Salvadoran embassy in country of residence, force majeure, or other unforeseen circumstances)
programs.processing_time: Not specified on official page; notification of approval/denial by email
programs.notes: Applied online at https://elsalvadorpei.migracion.gob.sv/. Fee: USD 100. Applicant creates an account, completes the form, uploads required documents, and pays online. Approval, conditional approval, denial, or cancellation is communicated by email. Contact: +503 7070-0663 / atencion.usuarios@migracion.gob.sv. Service page published November 14, 2023.

agreements: CA-4 Border Control Agreement (El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua) – free movement zone with shared 90-day entry period across the CA-4 region
Convenio de Creación de la Visa Única Centroamericana Para Libre Movilidad de Extranjeros (2005) – determines unified visa categories for CA-4 bloc

validation_notes: CORRECTED (2026-06-29): max_stay_days for Category A visa_free entry restored to 180 days. The 2026-06-29 re-validation found strong multi-source evidence for 180 days: (1) UK GOV.UK official travel advice (gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/el-salvador/entry-requirements): 'You can travel to El Salvador for tourism or business for up to 180 days without a visa'; (2) Canada travel.gc.ca official government advice: Canadian citizens can remain for up to 180 days without a visa; (3) Wikipedia Visa policy of El Salvador: 'up to 180 days' for the 89 visa-exempt jurisdictions. The previous 2026-06-27 downgrade to 90 days was incorrect — it confused El Salvador's individual 180-day grant with the CA-4 regional shared 90-day quota. The CA-4 quota governs movement within the CA-4 bloc as a whole; El Salvador can still grant 180 days as its own individual entry period for non-CA-4 Category A visitors. CA-4 entry (Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua nationals) remains at 90 days (shared CA-4 quota). Note: the US State Dept page may only cite the CA-4 context for US citizens; the broader 180-day figure is confirmed by GOV.UK and travel.gc.ca for their respective citizens.

CORRECTED (2026-06-27): The USD 12 tourist card (Tarjeta de Ingreso Migratorio, TIM) fee was eliminated by the Legislative Assembly of El Salvador in April/May 2025. The Director of Migration Ricardo Cucalón described the fee as 'incongruent with the country's goals of openness and investment.' The US State Dept page (updated June 25, 2026) no longer references any tourist card fee. fee_usd updated from 12 to 0 in visa_types and notes updated throughout. Source: elsalvadorinenglish.com (May 1, 2025, citing Legislative Assembly session).
CORRECTED: CA-4 visa_free entry max_stay_days updated from null to 90 (the CA-4 shared 90-day regional quota is the operative limit for Guatemala, Honduras, and Nicaragua nationals).

CORRECTED: CBI program payment now confirmed as BTC or USDt (Tether) - not exclusively Bitcoin. The official world.adoptingelsalvador.gob.sv page explicitly states 'BTC or USDt' as payment options. Added split payment structure: USD 999 non-refundable application fee + USD 999,001 upon approval.
CORRECTED: Processing time note updated from '4–6 weeks' to '~6 weeks' consistent with official site language.

CORRECTED: Taiwan removed from the excluded_nationalities list in conditional_access entries. El Salvador severed diplomatic ties with Taiwan in 2018 and recognizes only the People's Republic of China. The official RREE visa checker dropdown does not list Taiwan; it lists 'República Popular China'. Taiwan's exclusion was incorrect framing - Taiwanese passport holders would be classified under whatever category applies to them, not as a separately listed excluded nationality distinct from China.
CONFIRMED: Three visa categories (A/B/C) structure remains accurate and confirmed via rree.gob.sv/visas/ (scraped via Firecrawl stealth proxy - page previously returning HTTP 403 to direct WebFetch).
CONFIRMED: PEI (Special Entry Permit) at USD 100 remains active; service page confirmed updated November 14, 2023.
CONFIRMED: CBI Freedom Passport program (USD 1,000,000 minimum, 1,000/year quota) confirmed active as of mid-2026 via world.adoptingelsalvador.gob.sv.
CONFIRMED: Art. 152 definitive residence reform (Legislative Assembly, May 2025, USD 690 fee) confirmed via asamblea.gob.sv.

NOTE: rree.gob.sv/visas-para-extranjeros/ table is paginated and only showed rows 1–13 (Afganistán through Azerbaiyán) in both Firecrawl markdown and raw HTML scrapes. The full table could not be retrieved as a single document. Cross-referencing with Wikipedia's Visa policy of El Salvador and elsalvador.com news article (citing official DGME source) for broader country categorization.

CORRECTED (India, 2026-06-14): India (ordinary passport) is Category B = Visa Consular (visa required, obtained from a Salvadoran consulate before travel). India does NOT appear in visa_free, visa_on_arrival, eta, or e_visa arrays. Aggregate claims of visa-free or visa-on-arrival for India are FALSE. Confirmed from elsalvador.com article (Nov 2025, citing DGME) listing India explicitly under 'Países categoría B – requieren visa consular', cross-verified against Wikipedia 'Visa policy of El Salvador' (India not in the 89-jurisdiction visa-exempt list). India IS eligible for the substitute-visa (conditional_access) route if holding a valid US, Canada, or Schengen visa - India is NOT in the substitute-visa exclusion list. Source: https://www.elsalvador.com/turismo/cultura-viva/paises-visas-para-entrar-a-el-salvador/1250791/2025/ (citing DGME); https://rree.gob.sv/visas/
gaps: The distinction between the CA-4 shared 90-day regional quota and El Salvador's individual 180-day Category A grant needs further official clarification. GOV.UK notes that other CA-4 nations may only recognize the first 90 days even if El Salvador grants 180 days; this creates a practical ambiguity for travelers. The 180-day figure is confirmed for El Salvador individually; the CA-4 regional coordination around this is unclear.

Full enumeration of Category A (visa-exempt) countries not retrievable from a single official static document: the official list is a Google Drive-hosted file linked from migracion.gob.sv/servicios/listado-visa/ (published 2020-11-10) which requires direct download access.

Full enumeration of Category B (consular visa) and Category C (consulted visa) countries not independently verified from the official static DGME document. Non-official source (elsalvador.com, Nov 2025, citing DGME) reported ~41 Category C countries and ~65 Category B countries (India confirmed Category B). India's Category B status is now corroborated by both elsalvador.com/DGME and Wikipedia/RREE and is no longer an open gap.

The rree.gob.sv/visas-para-extranjeros/ table is rendered via JavaScript pagination and only the first 13 rows (A through Azerbaijan) were retrievable by automated scraping, preventing independent verification of the complete diplomatic/service passport waiver country list.
Minimum investment amount for the Definitive Residence (Art. 152) investor program not stated in any official source reviewed.
Dual citizenship rules for the Freedom Passport (CBI) not confirmed on the official adoptingelsalvador.gob.sv pages.

Digital nomad visa details (referenced in non-official sources at ~USD 2,825, 1-year renewable, income requirement ~USD 1,460/month) not confirmed from any official government domain. Not recorded here due to lack of official source.
The 5,000 free passports for scientists/artists sub-program under Adopting El Salvador lacks official documentation with specific eligibility criteria and application details.

rree.gob.sv/visas/ is a dynamic JavaScript form tool: the exact entry level output for each nationality+visa-type+credential combination could not be fully enumerated. The conditional_access entries for US/Canada/Schengen visa holders and legal residents abroad are confirmed as form inputs on the official tool, but the precise resulting entry level per nationality combination is not published in any static official document.
The specific countries whose legal residence permits qualify under the 'resides legally abroad' rule are not enumerated in any static official page found.

OAS/UN/European Commission travel document holder rules: the RREE tool lists these entities as passport issuers, but specific conditions and stay limits for their travel document holders are not in any retrievable static official page.
No OCI (Overseas Citizenship of India) or analogous special heritage/diaspora status rule found for El Salvador from any official source.

Taiwan: El Salvador does not recognize Taiwan diplomatically (recognition withdrawn 2018). Taiwanese passport holders are not listed as a separate category in any official source reviewed; their entry category under the ordinary visa framework was not verifiable from official sources.

visa_types.name: Admission for Category A Visa-Exempt Entry (formerly Tarjeta de Ingreso Migratorio)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay tourism, leisure, or family visits for nationals of visa-exempt (Category A) countries. Processed at the port of entry.
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of approximately 89 Category A (visa-exempt) jurisdictions. The USD 12 Tarjeta de Ingreso Migratorio (TIM) fee was eliminated by the Legislative Assembly of El Salvador in April/May 2025; as of 2026 entry is fee-free for Category A nationals. Standard stay is 180 days (confirmed by UK GOV.UK and Canada travel.gc.ca official government sources, June 2026). CA-4 nationals (Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua) enter under the CA-4 Agreement instead and share a 90-day regional quota. Note: the CA-4 shared 90-day quota applies to movement within the CA-4 bloc as a whole; El Salvador can grant up to 180 days as its own individual entry period.

visa_types.name: Visa Consular – Category B (Tourism/Short Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for nationals of Category B countries who require a consular visa, obtainable at a Salvadoran embassy or consulate without prior DGME authorization.
visa_types.notes: Category B countries require a consular visa (visa consular o sin consulta) obtained before travel from a Salvadoran embassy or consulate. The consular office grants the visa without needing prior approval from DGME. Approximately 65 countries fall into this category. Form F46 is the consulted visa form; Form F47 is the multiple non-resident visa (1–3 years).

visa_types.name: Visa Consultada – Category C (Tourism/Short Stay)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for nationals of Category C countries, which requires prior authorization from the General Directorate of Migration and Foreigners (DGME) before the consulate can issue the visa.
visa_types.notes: Approximately 41 countries are Category C. Applicants must first obtain DGME authorization (consulted visa, Form F46 or F49) before the consulate will issue the visa. The PEI (Special Entry Permit) online process is an alternative pathway for Category C nationals when no local Salvadoran consulate is accessible. Form F49 covers consulted visas specifically for investors, business persons, and commercial representatives.

visa_types.name: Permiso Especial de Ingreso/Tránsito/Salida para Marineros (Special Permit for Sailors)
visa_types.purpose: Special entry, transit, or exit permit for foreign sailors arriving by sea or air for crew purposes.
visa_types.notes: Form F50. Specific fees and duration not published on official page. Applied for through the DGME.

visa_types.name: Permiso Especial de Ingreso para Eventos de Artistas (Special Entry Permit for Artists)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign artists to enter El Salvador to perform at concerts, theater, dance, circus, or other cultural and commercial entertainment events.
visa_types.notes: Form F51. Fee: USD 60 for Central American artists, USD 100 for non-Central American artists. Processing: 5 business days after payment and document verification. Application submitted by email to peiartistas@migracion.gob.sv. Requires authorization from the General Directorate of Public Spectacles, artist roster with passport details, and travel itineraries. The sponsoring entity must be a Salvadoran-based person or company.

visa_types.name: Permiso Especial de Ingreso (PEI) – Special Entry Permit (Online)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals who require a visa but cannot access a Salvadoran consulate (due to lack of embassy, residency issues, or force majeure) to obtain entry authorization online.
visa_types.notes: Applied online at https://elsalvadorpei.migracion.gob.sv/. Eligibility: any foreign national whose nationality requires a visa (Category B or C) but who cannot obtain one through a Salvadoran consulate. Fee: USD 100. Outcome (approval, conditional approval, denial, or cancellation) sent by email. Not a substitute for a standard visa when consular access exists.

visa_types.name: Extensión de Estancia para No Residentes (Non-Resident Stay Extension)
visa_types.purpose: Extends the permitted stay of a non-resident foreign visitor beyond their initial authorized period.
visa_types.notes: Form F44 (adults) and F45 (children/adolescents). Extension up to 90 additional days. Specific fees not published on official page. Applied for at DGME offices.

visa_types.name: Tarjeta de Viajero Frecuente / Visa Múltiple No Residente para Inversionista, Empresario o Representante Comercial
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry non-resident visa or frequent traveler card for investors, business persons, and commercial representatives enabling repeated short-stay entries.
visa_types.notes: Forms F47 (Multiple Non-Resident Visa, 1–3 years) and F48 (Frequent Traveler Card for investors, business persons, or commercial representatives). Also F49 for consulted visa in this category. Fees and specific per-entry stay limits not published on official page. Designed for those who need to travel to El Salvador regularly for investment or commercial purposes without residing there.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F3 – Trabajador con Autorización (Temporary Residency with Work Authorization)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency status for foreign nationals who have received a job offer or employment contract in El Salvador and require authorization to work.
visa_types.notes: Form F3 for initial application; Form F20 for renewal. Issued as a residency card with a validity period specific to the category. Applicants submit to the DGME (General Directorate of Migration and Foreigners) with required employment documentation. Fees not published on official page. Renewable.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F5 – Empresario (Temporary Residency for Business)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals establishing or operating a business in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F5 for initial; Form F22 for renewal. Residency card issued with category-specific validity period. Fees not published on official page. Renewable.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F4 – Inversionista (Temporary Residency for Investors)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals making qualifying investments in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F4 for initial; Form F21 for renewal. Minimum investment amount not specified in any official document reviewed. Residency card issued with category-specific validity. Also available via Art. 152 reform (May 2025): investors in government-promoted programs may obtain definitive (permanent) residence directly, bypassing temporary residence, for a fee of USD 690.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F6 – Representante Comercial (Temporary Residency for Commercial Representatives)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals representing international commercial entities in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F6 for initial; Form F23 for renewal. Fees and specific duration not published on official page. Renewable.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F7 – Pensionado (Temporary Residency for Retirees)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign retirees receiving a pension who wish to reside in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F7 for initial; Form F24 for renewal. Minimum pension income requirement not specified in any official document reviewed. Fees and specific duration not published on official page. Renewable.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F8 – Rentista (Temporary Residency for Income-Based/Annuitant)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals who can demonstrate sufficient passive income or annuity to support themselves in El Salvador without employment.
visa_types.notes: Form F8 for initial; Form F25 for renewal. Distinct from the pensioner category (F7) - covers private income, investment income, or annuities rather than formal pension. Minimum income requirement not specified in any official document. Fees and duration not published on official page. Renewable.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F9 – Para Acompañar (Temporary Residency for Accompanying Dependents)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for family members (spouse, children) accompanying a principal foreign resident in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F9 for initial; Form F26 for renewal. The principal holder must already hold or be applying for a qualifying residency category. Fees and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F10 – Estudiante (Temporary Residency for Students)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals enrolled in an accredited educational institution in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F10 for initial; Form F27 for renewal. Requires enrollment documentation from a Salvadoran educational institution. Fees and specific duration not published on official page. Renewable as long as studies continue.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F1 – Transitoria (Transitory Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term transitional residency status for foreign nationals in specific transitional circumstances prior to obtaining a substantive residency category.
visa_types.notes: Form F1. Specific conditions, fees, and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F2 – Temporal (Standard Temporary Residency)
visa_types.purpose: General temporary residency status for foreign nationals not falling into other specific categories.
visa_types.notes: Form F2. A catch-all temporary residency category. Specific conditions, fees, and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F11 – Accionista (Temporary Residency for Shareholders)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals who are shareholders in Salvadoran companies.
visa_types.notes: Form F11 for initial; Form F28 for renewal. Distinct from the investor category (F4). Fees and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F12 – Comerciante Individual (Temporary Residency for Individual Merchants)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals operating as sole traders or individual merchants in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F12 for initial; Form F29 for renewal. Fees and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F13 – Cooperante (Temporary Residency for Aid/Cooperation Workers)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals working in El Salvador under international cooperation, development, or humanitarian aid agreements.
visa_types.notes: Form F13 for initial; Form F30 for renewal. Typically requires sponsorship from a recognized international organization or NGO operating in El Salvador. Fees and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F14 – Víctima de Trata (Temporary Residency for Trafficking Victims)
visa_types.purpose: Humanitarian temporary residency granted to foreign nationals identified as victims of human trafficking in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F14 for initial; Form F31 for renewal. Granted through official anti-trafficking identification processes. Fees waived or not applicable in humanitarian cases.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F15 – Razones Humanitarias (Temporary Residency for Humanitarian Reasons)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency granted to foreign nationals requiring protection on humanitarian grounds.
visa_types.notes: Form F15. Specific conditions, fees, and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Residencia Temporal F16 – Personal Religioso (Temporary Residency for Religious Personnel)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign religious workers, missionaries, clergy, and other religious personnel serving in El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Form F16. Requires documentation from the sponsoring religious institution. Fees and duration not published on official page.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official Passport Visa Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry for holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports from countries with bilateral agreements or arrangements with El Salvador.
visa_types.notes: Holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports from countries such as Belarus, Bolivia, Cuba, Dominica, Dominican Republic, Egypt, Grenada, Guyana, Haiti, Indonesia, Jamaica, Jordan, Kenya, Montenegro, Morocco, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Peru, Philippines, Serbia, Suriname, Thailand, Venezuela, Vietnam, and others (full list at rree.gob.sv) are visa-exempt. India diplomatic/official passport holders are visa-exempt for 90 days under a bilateral agreement (signed 2008, in force 2009). The RREE table is paginated and the full list was not retrievable in a single scrape.

outbound_official.note: No official El Salvador government outbound visa-list page located. The RREE (rree.gob.sv/visas/) operates an inbound visa classification tool for foreigners entering El Salvador, not an outbound list for Salvadoran citizens. The DGME (migracion.gob.sv/servicios/listado-visa/) publishes 'Países que requieren visa para ingresar a El Salvador' - also inbound. All third-party reports on visa-free access for Salvadoran passport holders cite Henley & Partners, not any official El Salvador government outbound page. Rely on destination-side data.
